# UC-17.1.1 · NAC Authentication Trending

## Description

Authentication success/failure trends reveal infrastructure issues (certificate problems, RADIUS outages) and security events.

## Value

Authentication success/failure trends reveal infrastructure issues (certificate problems, RADIUS outages) and security events.

## Implementation

Forward ISE PSN syslog (TCP/UDP 514) to a Heavy Forwarder. Configure `props.conf`/`transforms.conf` for field extraction of `AuthenticationPolicy`, `Passed/Failed`, `Calling-Station-ID`, and `NAS-IP-Address`. Alert on failure-rate spikes vs the 7-day same-hour baseline, segmented by `nas_ip` or `location`, to distinguish localized AP/switch issues from systemic RADIUS problems.

## Detailed Implementation

Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: `Splunk_TA_cisco-ise`, HPE Aruba ClearPass syslog, Forescout CounterACT syslog.
• Ensure the following data sources are available: RADIUS/ISE authentication logs.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Forward ISE PSN syslog (TCP/UDP 514) to a Heavy Forwarder. Configure `props.conf`/`transforms.conf` for field extraction of `AuthenticationPolicy`, `Passed/Failed`, `Calling-Station-ID`, and `NAS-IP-Address`. Alert on failure-rate spikes vs the 7-day same-hour baseline, segmented by `nas_ip` or `location`, to distinguish localized AP/switch issues from systemic RADIUS problems.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=nac sourcetype="cisco:ise:auth"
| eval status=if(match(message,"PASS"),"success","failure")
| timechart span=1h count by status
```

Understanding this SPL

**NAC Authentication Trending** — Authentication success/failure trends reveal infrastructure issues (certificate problems, RADIUS outages) and security events.

Documented **Data sources**: RADIUS/ISE authentication logs. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): `Splunk_TA_cisco-ise`, HPE Aruba ClearPass syslog, Forescout CounterACT syslog. The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: nac; **sourcetype**: cisco:ise:auth. If that sourcetype is not mentioned in Data sources, double-check parsing or update the documentation to match the feed you actually ingest.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=nac, sourcetype="cisco:ise:auth". Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **status** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• `timechart` plots the metric over time using **span=1h** buckets with a separate series **by status** — ideal for trending and alerting on this use case.

Step 3 — Validate
Compare with Cisco ISE Live Log and endpoint details for the same sessions, MACs, and time range.


Step 4 — Operationalize
Step 4 — Operationalize
Step 4 — Operationalize
Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Line chart (auth success/failure rates), Bar chart (failures by location), Pie chart (auth method distribution).

## SPL

```spl
index=nac sourcetype="cisco:ise:auth"
| eval status=if(match(message,"PASS"),"success","failure")
| timechart span=1h count by status
```

## CIM SPL

```spl
| tstats `summariesonly` count
  from datamodel=Authentication.Authentication
  by Authentication.user, Authentication.action, Authentication.src span=1h
| sort - count
```

## Visualization

Line chart (auth success/failure rates), Bar chart (failures by location), Pie chart (auth method distribution).
